raw_consumer_statement:
I made a purchase on XX/XX/year>, for {$150.00} from XXXX XXXX XXXX for access to the XXXX checkout automation software. The software was advertised as a tool to automate online purchases, but it did not function as described despite following all setup instructions. 

I contacted the merchant multiple times requesting assistance or a refund, but they refused, citing their Terms of Service ( TOS ). However, the TOS was not available for review on their website after purchase, making it impossible to verify their refund policy beforehand. 

I disputed the charge with Citibank, but my claim was denied because the merchant provided access logs showing that I logged in. However, logging in does not prove that the software worked as advertisedonly that I attempted to use the service. Citibank did not fully consider that the product was defective and that the refund policy was unfairly enforced. 

This business appears to have a pattern of selling software that does not work as advertised while refusing refunds under misleading terms. I am seeking assistance in having this dispute fairly reconsidered, as I was denied a refund for a service that failed to perform as promised.
